methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terra Drone, a public company listed on Tokyo exchanges, operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The target company completed its valuation upon IPO. The comparable universe within Drones & Autonomous comprises six private companies, including Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air. No public peers in the same sector were identified at comparable stages or valuation tiers.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable to this analysis. Despite five same-sector peers existing within Drones & Autonomous, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints across each narrow filter category. This insufficient subset size precluded reliable median-based valuation. A broader all-sector median approach would introduce material distortion given the specialized technical and commercial characteristics of autonomous systems development.

Given the limited comparability within the sector and the public listing status of Terra Drone, traditional triangulation methods are constrained. The analysis cannot generate a supported valuation range using available peer multiples. Key uncertainties include the lack of public comps to benchmark against and the tight set of private drone companies at similar developmental stages. Any valuation conclusion would require either: (1) acceptance of wider peer definitions at cost of comparability precision, or (2) reliance on alternative methods (DCF, precedent transactions) not reflected in the current dataset.
